questionFlow.note: Run the three scan agents in parallel and surface each report to the user.

questionFlow.prompt: Show the proposed split to the user and wait for confirmation before any blueprint-selector subagents are spawned.
questionFlow.note: Only the confirmed skill-worthy tech list is used for blueprint search.

questions.prompt: This is a monorepo project that contains multiple packages. Do you want one plugin for all of them, or one separate plugin for each?
Do you want one shared/core plugin to avoid duplication across packages?

questionFlow.note: Output levels 1-3 of the expected folder structure and wait for confirmation.

questionFlow.prompt: Which mode do you want to build your plugin with?
questionFlow.note: After mode selection, output the full folder structure including telemetry, validation, pre-commit, and CI. Wait for confirmation.
Spin one blueprint-selector subagent per confirmed skill-worthy tech in parallel.

questionFlow.prompt: Which AI platform do you want this plugin to target?
questionFlow.note: Claude Code and OpenCode include agents, other single-platform targets stay skill-first, and multi-platform generates the full caveman-style marketplace skeleton.

notes: Build the skeleton by scanning the GitHub repo itself for the matching platform skeletons.
Do not use the cached or installed plugin folder as the source of truth.
If GitHub cannot be reached, block and stop — no fallback.
When a single platform is selected, copy only the platform-specific folders and files needed for that surface.
When multi-platform is selected, compose the marketplace folder from the platform skeletons present in the GitHub repo and keep the platform-specific surfaces separate.
Do not bring in the old skeleton-detector path.
Generate skills and agents through the Agent tool, not inline.
Copy session-init.mjs, version-bump.mjs, and validate-plugins.mjs into the generated marketplace.
Generate a fresh pre-commit hook using the marketplace plugin folder name so the paths are correct.
Create the pilot.md command in the marketplace/commands folder from the pilot template and the selected mode workflow.
Run validation with node marketplace/plugins/core/scripts/validate-plugins.mjs marketplace/ before proceeding.
Instruct the user to install the created marketplace locally, then test the pilot command and iterate if concerns remain.
